name: Islamic Center of Irvine
mission: Mission statement - To establish a comprehensive center promoting Islamic values and reflecting excellence in serving the needs of the community
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2024 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.
zakatClaimEvidence: The Islamic Center of Irvine explicitly lists "Zakat Donations" as a donation option on its website. Furthermore, it has an "ICOI Zakat Fund" where "100% of the Islamic Center Of Irvine's Zakat fund goes towards providing zakat eligible members in the community". The organization also states, "If you would like to contribute funds to help needy families you can do so by either donating your Zakat ul-Maal or your Sadaqah."

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Early-stage organization. We show qualitative context while it builds a longer public track record.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 26 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

keyConcerns.headline: No outcome measurement or metrics tracking

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1)
components.improvement_suggestion: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

alignment.rationale: Alignment 21/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, religious cultural cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; high program ratio (≥85%) suggests efficient delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Document a clear theory of change with an explicit causal pathway.

dimension_explanations.alignment: As a Zakat-eligible institution, the center provides a medium fit for Muslim donors focusing on religious and cultural cause urgency [1]. Its track record is established through consistent community service, though data confidence remains at a medium level [2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high financial health, allocating an 89.9% program expense ratio toward its mission [2]. With 26.3 months of working capital and a fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised, it shows strong capacity-building potential, though specific cost-per-beneficiary data is not available [1][2].

In the 2022 fiscal year, the organization reported a total revenue of $2,209,994 while maintaining a high level of spending on its core mission activities [2].

rich_narrative.headline: The Islamic Center of Irvine Inc serves as a vital community hub dedicated to promoting Islamic values through comprehensive religious and social programming.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support a stable, financially healthy Islamic community hub that serves as both a direct service provider and a strategic funder for other local initiatives.
donor_motivations: Value long-term institutional stability and high liquidity
Want to fulfill zakat through a multi-service community center
Prefer organizations with zero fundraising overhead
Interested in supporting local Southern California Muslim infrastructure
giving_considerations: The organization acts as a major grantmaker to other centers
Financial reserves are high, indicating a focus on long-term sustainability
Specific impact data on individual beneficiaries is currently limited
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking international humanitarian relief or those who require rigorous, third-party academic impact evaluations of specific programs.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects strong financial health and verified program spending, but a lack of published outcome metrics or external evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The organization creates impact by providing religious services and educational programs while funding partner organizations to expand community reach.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the high programmatic spending is translating into meaningful social and religious outcomes for the Irvine community.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Despite strong reserves, the center's role as a grantmaker and its $2.2M revenue suggest it can absorb more capital to expand its community footprint.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="4">significant grantmaking capacity</cite> and <cite id="6">exceptionally high working capital reserves</cite>.

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="3">89.9% of its total expenses</cite> toward its core religious and community programs.
The center achieves <cite id="7">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, meaning donor funds are not depleted by solicitation costs.
With <cite id="6">26.3 months of working capital</cite>, the organization has significant financial resilience to sustain its mission through economic fluctuations.

strengths_deep_dive: The Islamic Center of Irvine's financial health is characterized by a <cite id="8">3-year revenue CAGR of 10.6%</cite>, demonstrating steady growth in community support. Its governance structure is stable, overseen by an <cite id="9">independent board of 9 members</cite> who ensure 100% independence in decision-making. The organization's transparency is reflected in its <cite id="10">Bronze Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, providing baseline visibility into its operations. Furthermore, its role as a grantmaker is substantial, with <cite id="11">domestic grants totaling over $2.1 million</cite> to partners like the Rahma Center and the American Islamic Institute of AV, effectively multiplying its impact through strategic local partnerships.
rich_narrative.summary: The Islamic Center of Irvine Inc (ICOI) serves as a central religious and social institution in Southern California, dedicated to <cite id="1">promoting Islamic values and reflecting excellence</cite> in community service. The organization operates with a significant financial footprint, reporting <cite id="2">total revenue of $2,209,994</cite> in the 2022 fiscal year. ICOI demonstrates a strong commitment to its core mission, maintaining a <cite id="3">program expense ratio of 89.9%</cite>, which indicates that the vast majority of its funding is directed toward religious and social programming rather than administrative overhead. Beyond its own operations, the center acts as a significant community funder, distributing <cite id="4">$2,125,949 in total grants</cite> to domestic recipients. This grantmaking activity is a cornerstone of its impact, with the <cite id="5">Rahma Center receiving over $1.8 million</cite> to support shared community objectives. The organization maintains <cite id="6">strong liquidity with 26.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing a robust buffer for long-term operational stability. Furthermore, the center operates with <cite id="7">exceptional fundraising efficiency at $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that donor contributions are utilized with maximum financial effectiveness.
